entities.name: GENDER AND SOCIAL TARGETING
entities.description: An approach to improve the relevance and effectiveness of plant breeding programs by focusing on specific social and gender groups

entities.name: PLANT BREEDING PROGRAMS
entities.description: Programs aimed at developing improved crop varieties to serve various stakeholders

entities.name: RESOURCE-POOR FARMERS
entities.description: Farmers with limited access to resources who are targets of plant breeding programs

entities.name: TRADERS
entities.description: Individuals or entities involved in the buying and selling of agricultural products

entities.name: PROCESSORS
entities.description: Entities that transform raw agricultural products into processed goods

entities.name: TRAIT PREFERENCES
entities.description: The specific characteristics of crops desired by different client groups

entities.name: SMALL-SCALE STUDIES
entities.description: Research conducted on a limited scale to gather information on client preferences

entities.name: BREEDING PRIORITIES
entities.description: The strategic focus areas for crop improvement based on client needs

entities.name: MARKETING APPROACH
entities.description: A strategy derived from marketing to better align plant breeding with client needs

entities.name: SEGMENTING-TARGETING-POSITIONING (STP) FRAMEWORK
entities.description: A consumer marketing framework adapted for gender and social targeting in plant breeding

entities.name: CONSUMER MARKETING
entities.description: The process of promoting and selling products to individual consumers

entities.name: BIOPHYSICAL VARIABLES
entities.description: Physical and biological factors relevant to crop production and targeting

entities.name: SOCIOECONOMIC VARIABLES
entities.description: Social and economic factors influencing the adoption and use of crop varieties

entities.name: NATIONAL LEVEL
entities.description: The scale of a country used for implementing targeting strategies
